title: Single owner bottleneck on backend integration
description: Too many integration tasks are assigned to one backend engineer, creating a queue and delay risk.
triggers: More than 40% tasks owned by one role
Critical path tasks all assigned to BE
mitigations: Rebalance ownership across BE and FE
Split integration tasks into parallel sub-tasks

title: Long dependency chain blocks delivery
description: Tasks form a long blocked_by sequence, so one delay cascades into multiple milestones.
triggers: Milestone includes tasks from same chain
mitigations: Add intermediate deliverables and integration checkpoints

title: Insufficient schedule buffer
description: Buffer days are below the typical 15% contingency threshold for project uncertainty.
mitigations: Reserve contingency for integration and QA

title: Optimistic QA effort estimate
description: Testing work is underestimated relative to scope, increasing defect escape and rework risk.
triggers: QA tasks under 10% of total effort
No regression test task in plan
mitigations: Add explicit test design and automation tasks

title: Milestones include unresolved blocked tasks
description: Milestone delivery criteria contain tasks still blocked by upstream work.
triggers: Target day assumes parallel work that is blocked

title: Cross-team handoff latency
description: Frequent FE-BE-QA handoffs introduce waiting time and coordination overhead.
mitigations: Plan overlap windows and handoff SLAs

title: Unclear API contract readiness
description: Frontend implementation starts before backend API contracts are finalized.
triggers: FE task depends on undefined API schema
No task for API contract sign-off
mitigations: Use mocked contract tests before integration

title: Critical path lacks parallelization
description: High-effort tasks on the critical path are planned sequentially even when partly independent.
triggers: No parallel streams in milestone plan
mitigations: Run infra and feature work in parallel

title: Deployment readiness omitted
description: No explicit DevOps or release-hardening activities are included before target release.
triggers: No rollback or observability checklist task
mitigations: Include staging validation and rollback drill

title: Data migration uncertainty
description: Schema or data migration complexity is not represented in estimates and milestones.

title: Third-party dependency volatility
description: External service limits or API changes can break timeline assumptions.
triggers: No fallback for external outage or rate limit
mitigations: Add retry and fallback design task

title: Security review scheduled too late
description: Security hardening and review happen near release, causing last-minute blockers.
mitigations: Add secure coding and scan checkpoints

title: Requirement-to-task coverage gaps
description: Some requirements have no mapped tasks, leading to hidden scope and missed outcomes.
triggers: Requirement IDs not referenced by tasks
mitigations: Add missing implementation and verification tasks

title: Ambiguous acceptance criteria increase rework
description: Vague acceptance criteria lead to mismatched implementation and QA expectations.
triggers: Criteria use words like fast or intuitive

title: No spike for unknown technical area
description: Novel architecture or technology is planned without feasibility validation.
triggers: New framework with no prior team experience
mitigations: Adjust estimates based on spike findings

title: Environment instability impacts QA
description: Shared test environments are unstable or unavailable, delaying verification.

title: Insufficient observability for go-live
description: Monitoring, logging, and alerting are not planned, slowing incident detection.

title: Review and approval lead time ignored
description: Business, security, or architecture approvals are not reflected in timeline.
triggers: Past projects had long review cycles
mitigations: Book reviewers early and track decision dates

title: Regression risk from compressed timeline
description: Shortened schedule reduces stabilization time and increases post-release defects.
mitigations: Reserve explicit regression and hardening time

title: Unplanned dependency on legacy system
description: Legacy integration behavior is poorly documented and causes surprise blockers.
mitigations: Add legacy discovery and contract tests

title: Scope creep via unbounded enhancement requests
description: New requests enter sprint without impact analysis, destabilizing milestones.
mitigations: Require trade-off decision before adding scope

title: Key person availability risk
description: Critical specialist availability is uncertain during key milestones.
triggers: Known PTO overlaps with release window
